_comment: WC2026 Round of 32, 2026-06-29, Houston Stadium. KNOCKOUT: no draw -- level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties, so the deliverable is who ADVANCES. Routes: Brazil won Group C (1-1 Morocco, 3-0 Haiti, 3-0 Scotland; 7 pts, GD +6, GA 1; rating 92) -- dominant and defensively tight. Japan finished 2nd in Group F (2-2 Netherlands, 4-0 Tunisia, 1-1 Sweden; 5 pts, GD +4, GF 7; rating 80) -- in form, technical and attacking, and crucially NOT a low block: they held the Netherlands and ran up 4 on Tunisia. So this is an OPEN, quality matchup, not a favourite-vs-bus tie -> backing Brazil + a live game (the Egypt-NZ / favourite+goals archetype), and do NOT under-rate Japan. KNOCKOUT-SPECIFIC: full motivation, no rotation; Japan's quality means they can take it to ET, so their advancement sits above a typical 80-rated underdog. Brazil the clear favourite by class (elite front line, GA 1 in the group). Home = Brazil (nominal; neutral venue). Cards not modelled (referee TBD).

meta.stakes: Single-elimination: level after 90' -> extra time -> penalties. Brazil the clear favourite by class; Japan in form, technical and attacking (held the Netherlands, beat Tunisia 4-0) -> an open game, not a low block, and a genuine threat to drag the tie to ET.

home.name: Brazil
notes: Elite (rating 92); dominant group, GA 1, top-class front line. attack 1.32.
Clear favourite; open game vs an attacking side, so not a low-scoring lock.

away.name: Japan
notes: In form (rating 80): held the Netherlands 2-2, beat Tunisia 4-0, drew Sweden. Technical and attacking -- NOT a low block. attack 1.05 (no under-rating).
Organized but will commit forward. defense 1.0.
Quality enough to take it to extra time -> advancement above a typical underdog.

_recal: First pass sat 9.3 pts BELOW the market on Brazil (49.2% vs 58.5%): respecting Japan's form is fair, but Japan being good does not override Brazil being ELITE -- the do-not-fade-an-elite-favourite lesson (Spain/Argentina). Lifted Brazil 1.62 -> 1.85 and trimmed Japan 1.08 -> 0.95 to align with the market while keeping the open-game read (still BTTS/Over live).

_lineups._comment: Confirmed/projected XIs (RotoWire/SI/ESPN, ~60 min pre-KO). Two changes, BOTH trimming attacks but Japan more: (1) Brazil are WITHOUT the injured Raphinha (a key wide threat); Ancelotti starts the young Rayan wide with Matheus Cunha central, and Neymar is fit only as a super-sub -> a slightly lighter Brazil attack, so 1.85 -> 1.78. (2) Japan lose their main creator Takefusa Kubo (ruled out) and set up in a cautious 3-4-2-1 (back three + wing-backs Doan/Nakamura), with Itakura doubtful -> a more contained, less creative Japan, so 0.95 -> 0.85. Net: Japan is weakened MORE than Brazil (Kubo is central to their play), so Brazil's edge nudges up a touch and the game projects lower-scoring (both missing creators, Japan sitting deeper). Brazil remain the clear favourite.
_lineups._confirmed: High-confidence: Brazil without Raphinha (injured), Neymar a super-sub, 4-3-3 with Rayan + Cunha + Vinicius. Japan without Kubo (ruled out), Itakura doubtful, a 3-4-2-1 with Ueda up top, Ito + Kamada as the two, Doan + Nakamura as wing-backs. The lambdas hinge on these (Raphinha out, Kubo out, Japan cautious) -- the exact Japan GK + back three are NOT confirmed in the sources and are left approximate; they do not move the model.
